meta.certificationName: Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Apps and Agents Developer Associate (AI-103)
meta.title: MS-AI103-6: Azure AI Foundry Agents, Retrieval, Evaluation, and Responsible AI

domains.name: Azure AI Foundry and Application Development

questions.stem: A project team needs to make a certification-aligned design decision about choosing Azure AI Foundry to build and manage a generative AI application. What is the BEST choice?

options.text: Use the managed capability or documented pattern for choosing Azure AI Foundry to build and manage a generative AI application, then validate it with realistic workload or evaluation data
Choose the largest model or compute shape for every workload and ignore latency, governance, and cost constraints
Move the decision entirely to end users at runtime without documenting defaults, limits, or fallback behavior
Treat the feature as complete after a local prototype, without testing service integration or operational behavior

questions.explanation: A is correct because the supporting documentation describes choosing Azure AI Foundry to build and manage a generative AI application as an explicit design or operational concern that should be configured and validated. B is incorrect because blindly maximizing resources ignores fit-for-purpose architecture. C is incorrect because runtime ambiguity creates inconsistent and hard-to-govern behavior. D is incorrect because a prototype alone does not prove production readiness. Reference: [What is Azure AI Foundry](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/what-is-azure-ai-foundry)

questions.stem: A team is designing a solution involving deploying an Azure OpenAI model for an application endpoint. Which TWO actions best align with recommended practice for this objective? (Select TWO)

options.text: Apply deploying an Azure OpenAI model for an application endpoint deliberately and verify the behavior with representative test cases
Rely on manual, undocumented steps because managed AI and ML services infer the intended production behavior automatically
Monitor the outcome of deploying an Azure OpenAI model for an application endpoint with service metrics, logs, evaluations, or review evidence appropriate to the risk
Bypass access controls and validation temporarily so the workflow can collect more context from every available source

questions.explanation: A and C are correct because the referenced documentation supports intentional configuration of deploying an Azure OpenAI model for an application endpoint and operational verification. B is incorrect because production AI and ML systems require explicit, repeatable design rather than undocumented manual behavior. D is incorrect because broad bypass of controls conflicts with secure and governed implementation. Reference: [What is Azure AI Foundry](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/what-is-azure-ai-foundry)

questions.stem: A project team needs to make a certification-aligned design decision about summarizing long conversations to stay within a context window. What is the BEST choice?

options.text: Use the managed capability or documented pattern for summarizing long conversations to stay within a context window, then validate it with realistic workload or evaluation data
Choose the largest model or compute shape for every workload and ignore latency, governance, and cost constraints
Move the decision entirely to end users at runtime without documenting defaults, limits, or fallback behavior

questions.stem: A team is designing a solution involving removing stale context that can distract the model. Which TWO actions best align with recommended practice for this objective? (Select TWO)

options.text: Apply removing stale context that can distract the model deliberately and verify the behavior with representative test cases
Rely on manual, undocumented steps because managed AI and ML services infer the intended production behavior automatically
Monitor the outcome of removing stale context that can distract the model with service metrics, logs, evaluations, or review evidence appropriate to the risk
Bypass access controls and validation temporarily so the workflow can collect more context from every available source

questions.explanation: A and C are correct because the referenced documentation supports intentional configuration of removing stale context that can distract the model and operational verification. B is incorrect because production AI and ML systems require explicit, repeatable design rather than undocumented manual behavior. D is incorrect because broad bypass of controls conflicts with secure and governed implementation. Reference: [Azure OpenAI prompt engineering concepts](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/concepts/prompt-engineering)
